channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: REBECCA GRANT: How Tomahawks work and how they could change everything for Ukraine
item.description: Trump weighs sending 1000-mile range Tomahawk cruise missiles to Ukraine, which could target Russian airbases and energy infrastructure across Russia.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ciattarelli gains momentum in New Jersey governor’s race as polls narrow sharply
item.description: Republican Jack Ciattarelli narrows gap with Democratic Rep. Mikie Sherrill in New Jersey governor race as new polls show tightening contest ahead of Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Israel radicals from 'global intifada' movement join 'No Kings' protests
item.description: Palestinian solidarity activists plan to embed in 'No Kings' protests nationwide as investigators examine nonprofit organizations for possible tax violations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In AP interview, Harris says Democrats 'are standing up for working people' in government shutdown
item.description: BIRMINGHAM, Ala. (AP) — As Democrats dig in for a lengthening government shutdown, former Vice President Kamala Harris is cheering them on as she travels the country touting her presidential campaign memoir amid speculation about another White House run.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After Voting Rights Act case arguments, concerns over diminished minority representation rise
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the faces of American politics have grown more diverse by nearly every measure, especially as racial minority communities gained political representation after longtime legal disenfranchisement and violent discrimination.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No SNAP benefits to be issued in November as government shutdown stretches on
item.description: Local governments are warning citizens that because the federal government has been shut down for weeks with no end in sight, November SNAP benefits will not be issued.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: First Republican enters race for governor of New Mexico in 2026 as Democrat terms out of office
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — New Mexico has its first Republican contender for governor ahead of the 2026 elections, as the three-term city mayor of fast-growing Rio Rancho launched his campaign.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump offers US automakers additional relief from his tariffs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is giving domestic automakers additional relief from tariffs on auto parts, extending what was supposed to have been a short-term rebate until 2030.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President Donald Trump commutes former New York GOP Rep. George Santos' prison sentence
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Friday he had signed the commutation of disgraced former Rep. George Santos after he was sentenced to 87 months in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US has seized survivors after strike on suspected drug-carrying vessel in Caribbean, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States took survivors into custody after the military struck a suspected drug-carrying vessel in the Caribbean, a defense official and another person familiar with the matter said Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada judge allows 'fake electors' case to move forward
item.description: The Nevada Attorney General's Office’s case against six so-called Republican “fake electors” will move forward after a judge’s ruling, a spokesperson confirmed Friday.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow National Guard in Chicago
item.description: Trump is requesting the Supreme Court toss out an order by a lower court judge that prevented the Guard from coming to the city for at least two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Obama continues tradition of slow-rolled political endorsements as gubernatorial elections hit fever pitch
item.description: Former President Barack Obama officially backed both Democrats running in this year's only gubernatorial elections in New Jersey and Virginia after staying mum on the races.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump drops f-bomb in Zelensky meeting about Maduro
item.description: "He doesn't want to f‑‑‑ with the U.S.," Trump said, speaking from the White House during a lunch meeting with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: New York GOP shuts down state's Young Republicans over group chat
item.description: The New York Republican State Committee’s leaders unanimously voted to disband the Empire State’s Young Republicans chapter on Friday after screenshots from a Telegram chat exposed members using antisemitic and racist language.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about federal charges against former national security adviser John Bolton
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton turned himself in to federal authorities Friday morning in Maryland and pleaded not guilty after being indicted on charges of keeping top secret documents at his home and sharing classified information with family members.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson shutters House again in bid to keep shutdown heat on Schumer
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson extends House recess through Oct. 23, maintaining pressure on Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer as the government shutdown continues.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawyers guild's alleged ties to Antifa spark call for DOJ probe
item.description: A Texas Republican requested a DOJ probe of the National Lawyers Guild's financing and support for Antifa extremists amid the Trump administration's crackdown on the group.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A war on drugs or a war on terror? Trump's military pressure on Venezuela blurs the lines
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Under President Donald Trump, the drug war is looking a lot like the war on terror. To support strikes against Latin American gangs and drug cartels, the Trump administration is relying on a legal argument that gained traction after the Sept. 11, 2001, attacks, which allowed U.S.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, ERIC TUCKER and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats say Trump needs to be involved in shutdown talks. He's shown little interest in doing so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is showing little urgency to broker a compromise that would end the government shutdown, even as Democrats insist no breakthrough is possible without his direct involvement. Three weeks in, Congress is at a standstill.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI and SEUNG MIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increased US pressure on Venezuela raises specter of regime change
item.description: President Trump's authorization of covert CIA strikes in Venezuela raises concerns of regime change and potential blowback from Caracas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: DNC chair: 'I believe that most Virginians have accepted' Jay Jones apology
item.description: Democratic National Committee (DNC) Chair Ken Martin said in an interview published Friday he believes most Virginians have accepted the apology from the party’s state attorney general nominee Jay Jones for recently unearthed violent text messages.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump ties crime with immigration, blurring the lines with Guard deployment
item.description: As President Trump pushes to get National Guard troops patrolling American cities, his administration has, in effect, blurred the lines between the military, traditional law enforcement and immigration enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf and Sergio Martínez-Beltrán, MPR News

item.title: Senators will force a vote to prevent war on Venezuela
item.description: Democratic Sen. Tim Kaine of Virginia says new legislation is needed to prevent the Trump administration’s escalating actions against Venezuela from leading to war.
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales, MPR News

item.title: After Zelenskyy meeting, Trump calls on Ukraine and Russia to ‘stop where they are’ and end the war
item.description: President Donald Trump is calling on Kyiv and Moscow to “stop where they are” and end their brutal war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Megyn Kelly says she'd run for president to stop Ocasio-Cortez
item.description: Conservative pundit Megyn Kelly said Thursday she could be motivated to run for president if Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) made her own White House bid. Laying into the progressive lawmaker on her popular podcast and radio show on Thursday, Kelly exclaimed "eff that woman.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, NewsNation

item.title: Florida congresswomen address food assistance programs amid shutdown
item.description: Federal officials are warning state agencies that benefits could be "delayed" or "interrupted" next month, if the shutdown continues.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lombardo: Resilient Nevada fuel supply is a homeland security issue for consumers, businesses
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo wants to use the Nevada Commission on Homeland Security to eliminate risks to the state's fuel supply, which currently relies heavily on California refineries and pipelines.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida bill ignites debate: Teachers to pledge academic oath
item.description: A new bill just filed at the statehouse would make Florida educators take an oath to uphold the constitution and state laws before ever stepping foot into a classroom. It's similar to what lawyers, doctors, and public officials pledge.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he plans to meet Putin again as Ukraine war drags
item.description: President Donald Trump says he will meet with the Russia president in Budapest, after high level meetings next week that would include Secretary of State Marco Rubio.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Video: Sen. McConnell falls walking to Capitol for votes
item.description: (NewsNation) — Sen. Mitch McConnell, R-Ky., fell as he walked to the Capitol for votes on Thursday. It happened in the basement of the Russell Senate office building. The video shows McConnell holding an aide's hand as he walks.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Legal experts say Kristi Noem’s airport video breaks the law. Penalties are unlikely
item.description: In a Department of Homeland Security video, Kristi Noem blames Democrats for the government shutdown. Law and ethics experts say it violates the Hatch Act, but there are rarely serious consequences.
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz, MPR News

item.title: Health insurance premiums are going up next year
item.description: As U.S. health insurance costs rise, some companies are paying for all of their workers' premiums. It's a big expense — but they say it pays off.
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court hears case that questions major plank of voting rights
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday hears a case that could strike down the last major part of the 1965 Voting Rights Act that remains standing.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg and Alyssa Kapasi, MPR News

item.title: Dwindling paychecks add pressure on GOP, Trump, Democrats to end shutdown
item.description: Pressure on lawmakers to reach a deal to end the government shutdown is expected to rise this week with the prospect that workers will begin to lose paychecks, including members of the military.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, WFLA, NBC
